extraction.one_line_summary: PINOT attacked inside 500m to win atop Tourmalet, ALAPHILIPPE took second plus bonuses to extend GC lead while THOMAS lost 36 seconds and yellow jersey hopes.
tactical_patterns: QUINTANA cracked 6.5 miles from summit when his own teammate SOLER set tempo, revealing Movistar prioritizes internal hierarchy over protecting GC riders.
THOMAS isolated when INEOS paced for BERNAL instead, showing team already shifted yellow jersey support despite defending champion still being competitive.
PINOT delayed sprint until 500m not at steeper gradients earlier, suggesting he calculated ALAPHILIPPE's sprint superiority required minimal run-in distance.
ALAPHILIPPE defended yellow by following wheels rather than attacking, inverting traditional GC strategy where leaders avoid energy expenditure on stage wins.

rider_signals.PINOT Thibaut: positive: Won stage with late acceleration and moved up GC, demonstrating both tactical patience and finishing speed on HC summit.
rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: positive: Extended GC lead over two minutes despite defending on climbs, proving time trial gains translate to sustained yellow jersey viability.
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: negative: Lost 36 seconds and team support to BERNAL, isolated on final climb suggesting INEOS already writing off defending champion's GC chances.
rider_signals.QUINTANA Nairo: negative: Cracked 10.5km from summit when teammate SOLER paced, catastrophic collapse indicates fundamental form deficit not tactical misjudgment.
rider_signals.BERNAL Egan: positive: INEOS prioritized pacing for him over defending champion THOMAS, signaling internal team decision on GC leadership already made.
rider_signals.KRUIJSWIJK Steven: positive: Finished third on Tourmalet summit despite not traditionally being strongest stage finisher, revealing improved peak climbing form.
rider_signals.BUCHMANN Emanuel: positive: Finished fourth and stayed with elite group to summit, confirming genuine GC contender status beyond early race positioning.
rider_signals.SOLER Marc: neutral: Paced so hard for Movistar he dropped teammate QUINTANA, exposing team's rigid adherence to predetermined tempo regardless of circumstances.
